destinations.evidence: The SPC verifier imports the shared ritual_spc_signature implementation and both direct verification paths delegate to it with the complete executor/job/context argument set.

equivalence_argument: The local verifier no longer reconstructs or verifies signatures independently; shared-core false or error results are returned as SPC verification failure paths.
notes: Mapped to ritual-spc-verification in the port.
rationale: The primary diff removes the local secp256k1/json reconstruction implementation, imports ritual_spc_signature::verify_signature_core, and exposes a local wrapper that delegates all cryptographic verification to the shared crate. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: SPC direct signature verification must call the shared ritual_spc_signature::verify_signature_core with the executor public key, block number, transaction hash, precompile address, precompile input, output, previous block metadata, error string, and proof; invalid shared-core results must cause SPC verification failure.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create SPC verification requests with a valid proof, a too-short proof, and a proof signed over altered output; assert verify_signature_direct_sync and the public verify_signature_core wrapper agree with ritual_spc_signature::verify_signature_core for all cases.
summary: SPC direct verification delegates to the shared signature verifier.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Compare verify_signature_direct_sync and ritual_spc_signature::verify_signature_core on valid, short-proof, and altered-output fixtures and assert matching outcomes.
title: SPC signatures use shared verifier
